spots.title: Wire the deployment manifest to the DeepBook SDK client
spots.prompt:
The deployment manifest has been fetched. Now you need to wire it into the DeepBook SDK client.

Your task: implement the three helper functions (`packageIds`, `coinMap`, `poolMap`) that extract the SDK configuration from the manifest, then build the `SuiGrpcClient` and attach the `deepbook` extension.

You are configuring for pool: {{ pool_subset }}.

Lines 39–58 of `src/App.tsx` are the target. Use `packageIds(manifest)`, `coinMap(manifest)`, and `poolMap(manifest)` as arguments to the `deepbook(...)` call.

phases.title: Phase 2 — Resilient gRPC Calls with withRetry

spots.title: Implement the withRetry helper for resilient DeepBook gRPC simulation calls
spots.prompt:
The DeepBook gRPC simulation calls can fail transiently. Your task is to implement a `withRetry` helper that wraps a promise-returning function with automatic retry logic.

Implement `withRetry` at lines 103-114 of `src/App.tsx`. The helper should:
- Accept a function that returns a Promise
- Retry up to a configurable number of times on failure
- Use exponential back-off between retries
- Re-throw the last error if all retries are exhausted

This will be used to wrap the `simulatePlaceMarketOrder` and `simulatePlaceLimitOrder` calls in the polling loop.

phases.title: Phase 3 — Polling Loop with Failure Tolerance

spots.title: Implement the polling tick that fetches mid price and level 2 ticks from DeepBook
spots.prompt:
Implement the polling loop that periodically fetches order book data from DeepBook using the `withRetry` helper you built in phase 2.

Your task: implement the polling tick at lines 116-145 of `src/App.tsx`. The tick should:
- Call `deepbook.getLevel2TicksFromMid` to fetch mid price and ticks
- Use `withRetry` to wrap the DeepBook call for resilience
- Update the component state with the fetched data
- Poll every {{ poll_interval_ms }} milliseconds using `setInterval`
- Handle errors gracefully by logging them without crashing the UI

The polling interval is {{ poll_interval_ms }}ms as configured in your personalization settings.
